headline: Cyril Imhof returns repeatedly to the agentic adoption gap, arguing AI agents now outpace both RAG chatbots and slow-moving incumbents.
body: Imhof's shorts cluster around one thread: agentic AI is advancing faster than businesses can integrate it, widening a competitive adoption gap [3][10], with agents like Claude beating RAG chatbots on file management, spreadsheets, and communications [1]. He attributes the speed of firms like Anthropic to organizational agility and the absence of legacy systems [2], credits OpenAI's edge to early compute and data center investment [5][8], and predicts small open-source models will run locally on consumer hardware within 18 months to 2 years, keeping sensitive Excel data off cloud services like Azure [4][7]. Claude formalizes the agent trend with Claude Tag, an agent identity model that lets Claude act under its own scoped identity in Slack with admin governance and full auditability [13]. On the teaching side, Caleb Writes Code walks visually through GPT internals including self-attention, multi-head attention, feed-forward networks, embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections [0].
